name: Very Special Pear Marmalade
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the pears in a heavy stainless steel or enamel pan, preferably.
Add sugar and lemon juice. Bring to a boil and stir until the sugar has dissolved, about 5 minutes.
2. Dice the pieces of preserved ginger to match the pear pieces as closely as possible.
Add the syrup and the ginger to the pears.
3. Simmer until the pears become transparent and turn the color of the ginger, 20-25 minutes.
Do not take the temperature higher than 220 degrees on the candy thermometer; it will thicken more as it cools.
4. Spoon into sterilized jars or small jars. Cool and refrigerate.
Or spoon into freezer jars, leaving 1 inch space. Cool and freeze. Makes 3 pints.
The pears and ginger don't have to be precise, but it does look prettier.
Select hard pears such as Kieffer or Seckel.
